questions.q: B.N. Rau served the Constituent Assembly of India in which capacity?
questions.explanation: Sir Benegal Narsing Rau was the Constitutional Adviser to the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: What was B.N. Rau's principal contribution to the making of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: He prepared the initial draft of the Constitution as Constitutional Adviser.

questions.q: After his constitutional work, B.N. Rau served as a judge of which international body?
questions.explanation: He later served as a judge of the International Court of Justice.

questions.q: What do the initials B.N. in B.N. Rau stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Benegal Narsing Rau.

questions.q: B.N. Rau is best described as a:
questions.explanation: He was a distinguished jurist and civil servant.

questions.q: B.N. Rau was honoured with which British title, reflecting his pre-independence service?
questions.explanation: He was knighted and is often referred to as Sir Benegal Narsing Rau.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly that B.N. Rau advised was tasked with:
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly was tasked with drafting the Constitution of India, and Rau advised it.

questions.q: B.N. Rau prepared the initial draft, which was then examined by which body of the Assembly?
questions.explanation: His initial draft was reviewed and refined by the Drafting Committee of the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: B.N. Rau came from a distinguished family of which present-day Indian region?
questions.explanation: He came from a notable family from the coastal Karnataka region of South India.

questions.q: Before drafting India's Constitution, B.N. Rau had a long career in which service?
questions.explanation: He had a distinguished career in the Indian Civil Service before his constitutional work.

questions.q: In which year was B.N. Rau appointed Constitutional Adviser to the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: He was appointed Constitutional Adviser in 1946, as the Assembly began its work.

questions.q: In which year was B.N. Rau born?
questions.explanation: Benegal Narsing Rau was born on 26 February 1887.

questions.q: To prepare his draft, B.N. Rau studied the constitutions of several countries. This comparative study was a hallmark of his role as:
questions.explanation: As Constitutional Adviser he studied many national constitutions to inform his draft.

questions.q: In which year did B.N. Rau pass away?
questions.explanation: B.N. Rau died on 30 November 1953.

questions.q: B.N. Rau was elected a judge of the International Court of Justice, which sits in which city?
questions.explanation: The International Court of Justice, on which he served, sits at The Hague in the Netherlands.

questions.q: Before independence, B.N. Rau served as a judge of which Indian court?
questions.explanation: He served as a judge of the Calcutta High Court before independence.

questions.q: B.N. Rau also served as India's representative at which world body before his ICJ role?
questions.explanation: He served as India's permanent representative to the United Nations before joining the ICJ.

questions.q: The Constitution that B.N. Rau helped draft was adopted by the Constituent Assembly in which year?
questions.explanation: The Constitution he helped draft was adopted on 26 November 1949.

questions.q: B.N. Rau's draft served as the working basis later refined by the Drafting Committee chaired by:
questions.explanation: His initial draft was taken forward by the Drafting Committee chaired by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: B.N. Rau's role as Constitutional Adviser was an official, non-elected position. This distinguishes him from the Assembly's:
questions.explanation: He advised in an official capacity and was not an elected member of the Assembly.

questions.q: B.N. Rau had earlier helped reform and codify laws relating to which area of personal law?
questions.explanation: He played an important role in the early efforts to reform and codify Hindu personal law.

questions.q: B.N. Rau was elected to the International Court of Justice in which year?
questions.explanation: He was elected a judge of the International Court of Justice in 1952.

questions.q: B.N. Rau served as the Prime Minister or chief executive officer (Dewan) of which princely state in the mid-1940s?
questions.explanation: He served as Prime Minister of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir during the mid-1940s (around 1944-1945).

questions.q: B.N. Rau studied at which English university as a young man?
questions.explanation: He studied at the University of Cambridge in England.

questions.q: B.N. Rau's draft of the Indian Constitution was prepared after he toured several countries to consult constitutional experts. One country he visited was:
questions.explanation: He travelled to countries including the United States to consult jurists while preparing the draft.

questions.q: At the United Nations, B.N. Rau served as President of the Security Council for a period during which year?
questions.explanation: As India's representative, he presided over the UN Security Council in 1950.

questions.q: B.N. Rau's elder brother, also a noted figure, served as the Governor of which central institution?
questions.explanation: His brother Sir Benegal Rama Rau served as a Governor of the Reserve Bank of India.

questions.q: B.N. Rau died in 1953 while still holding which office?
options: Judge of the International Court of Justice
questions.explanation: He passed away in 1953 while serving as a judge of the International Court of Justice.

questions.q: B.N. Rau entered the Indian Civil Service after qualifying in which decade?
questions.explanation: He joined the Indian Civil Service in the first decade of the twentieth century.

questions.q: The comparative groundwork B.N. Rau laid as Constitutional Adviser is often credited with shaping which feature borrowed into the Indian Constitution?
options: Procedural and structural provisions drawn from various constitutions
questions.explanation: His comparative study informed many procedural and structural provisions drawn from constitutions around the world.
